_comment: Pinned config for the grader. Weights and rubric definitions follow GRADING_METRICS_MAP.md. The weights are the /100 split per track (vibe = 9 rubrics, non_ai = 7). LLM-scored rubrics run ONCE at temperature 0 (self_consistency_n = 1) and return ONE holistic 1-10 score plus a strong/weak/missing verdict per criterion — never a number per criterion. The backend multiplies every 1-10 score by 10 once, so the page reads a single 0-100 scale and does no arithmetic.

tests.title: Does the code work?
tests.label: tests
tests.good: Passes every hidden check — the basic behaviour, the core mechanism under load, and the edge cases.
tests.bad: Tests fail — wrong results, races, or breakage at the boundaries.

traps.title: Did they fix the planted bugs?
traps.label: traps
traps.good: The deliberately planted bugs are caught and fixed.
traps.bad: The planted bugs are left in the code.

code_quality.title: Is the final code clean and correct?
code_quality.label: code quality
code_quality.good: Correct, idiomatic, readable, handles edge cases, no new defects.
code_quality.bad: Incorrect, awkward, unclear, misses edge cases, or introduces defects.

criteria.correctness: Does it work — passes tests, fixes traps.
criteria.idiomatic: Uses the language the normal way — standard library and conventions.
criteria.no_ai_defects: No new races, hallucinated APIs, leaked secrets, or needless abstractions.

architectural_reasoning.title: Did they make sound design decisions?
architectural_reasoning.label: architectural reasoning
architectural_reasoning.good: Weighed trade-offs before coding, got this challenge's core mechanism right, thought about boundaries, kept it simple.
architectural_reasoning.bad: No rationale, the core mechanism is unsafe or missing, ignores constraints, or over-engineered.

criteria.algorithm_choice: Picked a sensible algorithm (credited only if the candidate chose it, not the starter).
criteria.data_structure_choice: Picked a sensible data structure (credited only if the candidate chose it, not the starter).
criteria.core_mechanism_design: Got the central hard part of THIS challenge right — lock placement for a cache, monotonic-time correctness for a TTL, backpressure for a rate limiter, and so on.
criteria.not_over_engineered: Kept it as simple as the problem needs.

challenge_specific.title: Did they handle this challenge's specifics?
challenge_specific.label: challenge-specific
challenge_specific.good: Reaches for the right primitive and keeps the small, challenge-specific calls correct.
challenge_specific.bad: Misses the challenge-specific call — wrong primitive or a detail that won't compile/run correctly.

verification_discipline.title: Did they check their own work?
verification_discipline.label: verification discipline
verification_discipline.good: Runs the tests as they go, reviews changes instead of trusting them blindly, and runs the tests once more before submitting.
verification_discipline.bad: Never runs the tests, accepts code without checking, or submits untested.

developer_signal.title: Did they behave like a developer?
developer_signal.label: developer signal
developer_signal.good: Opens and reads the relevant files, and runs the tests.
developer_signal.bad: Minimal engagement — barely opens files and never runs tests.

ai_judgment.title: Did they judge the AI's output?
ai_judgment.label: AI judgment
ai_judgment.good: Rejects wrong AI suggestions, edits AI code after accepting it, recovers from bad changes.
ai_judgment.bad: Accepts everything the AI produces without question.

llm_communication.title: Did they instruct the AI clearly?
llm_communication.label: LLM communication
llm_communication.good: Gives the AI the context it needs, states constraints, breaks work into focused prompts, and gives specific feedback when the AI is wrong.
llm_communication.bad: Vague one-liners like "fix the bugs" with no context and no follow-up.

criteria.context_framing: Gave the AI the context it needed, including attaching the relevant files (@-mention / pin) when useful.
criteria.decomposition: Broke the work into focused prompts.
criteria.iterative_refinement: Built on previous answers instead of restarting.
criteria.debug_loop: Gave specific feedback when the AI was wrong.
criteria.token_discipline: Kept prompts efficient vs the budget.

debugger.title: Debugger usage
debugger.good: Started the debugger (and used code navigation) to investigate the problem — rewarded most heavily of all the developer signals.
debugger.bad: Nothing counts as bad — skipping the debugger carries no penalty.

product_sense.title: Product-sense bonus
product_sense.good: Picks a real user/operator need, builds a safe, tested change for it, and writes a clear NOTES.md.
product_sense.bad: Nothing counts as bad — skipping the bonus carries no penalty.

criteria.thread_safe: Kept any new state safe under concurrency.
criteria.proven_by_tests: Showed the new behaviour works with tests.

prompt_classification_ladder.5: Model-native: minimal-but-sufficient context, structured prompts, 1-2 tries
prompt_classification_ladder.4: Professional: cites exact errors, types, line numbers; 2-3 iterations max
prompt_classification_ladder.3: Competent: clear problems, some context, occasional back-and-forth
prompt_classification_ladder.2: Naive: generic descriptions, over-relies on model to figure out intent
prompt_classification_ladder.1: Counterproductive: massive dumps, 'fix this', no context
